text: <par>There is a homophonous WB word <latinform>ʔəkhyâŋ</latinform> 'one who is connected with another', cognate to Lahu <latinform>ɔ̀-chɔ̂</latinform> 'friend' (HPTB p. 265). it is possible that there is a genuine semantic assocation between these two WB words, with the placenta conceived of as the "child's friend".</par>
<par>This word is glossed "weighted bamboo screen, pushed to drive fish into a weir" (<emph>Myanmar-English Dictionary</emph> 1993:45).</par>
<par>Burmese <latinform>krim</latinform> was assigned to <xref ref="6174">#6174</xref> in HPTB:305 but it now seems preferable to include it under the present root.</par>

<par>Also glossed as "inside of mouth". Possibly allofamic with <latinform>ak</latinform>, although a WB alternation between open <latinform>a</latinform> and <latinform>ak</latinform> is not attested elsewhere.</par>
